attributes.description: Alerts when backend-sourced buffer writes exceed 100 in the window. Background writer cannot keep up — write pressure.
attributes.name: [PostgreSQL OTel] Backend buffer writes high

investigation_guide.blob:
## PostgreSQL backend buffer writes high

### What fired
`postgresql.bgwriter.buffers.writes` with `source=backend` summed > 100 in the window.

### Why it matters
When the bgwriter cannot keep up, backends must flush dirty buffers themselves — hurting query latency. Sustained backend writes predict I/O contention.

### Triage
1. Inspect `bgwriter.maxwritten` stops.
2. Check checkpoint frequency.

### Remediation
- Tune bgwriter parameters.
- Upgrade disk IOPS.

### Tuning
- Threshold 100; tune per scrape cadence.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-postgresqlreceiver.otel-*
// Buffers written by backend (not bgwriter) indicate background writer cannot keep up
| WHERE postgresql.bgwriter.buffers.writes IS NOT NULL AND attributes.source == "backend"
| EVAL writes_val = TO_DOUBLE(postgresql.bgwriter.buffers.writes)
| STATS backend_writes = SUM(writes_val) BY service.instance.id
// Threshold: > 100 buffers in window; adjust based on checkpoint frequency
| WHERE backend_writes > 100
